objective: Test unconventional hypotheses: cifra negra, crime heterogeneity, quantile effects, stationarity, novel combos

results.title: Cifra negra: adjusted vs raw IDDE→crime
results.description: Adjusting crime rates for underreporting using perception as proxy
results.finding: Raw r=0.5984 (p=0.0004). Adjusted r=0.1863 (p=0.3157). Δ=-0.4121. Underreporting inflates the IDDE-crime correlation.
results.recommendation: If delta is large, the dashboard MUST acknowledge cifra negra as a confounder.

results.title: IDDE → Cyber crime group
results.description: Composite rate of cyber crimes vs IDDE
results.finding: Cyber: r=0.6286, p=0.0002. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
results.recommendation: If Cyber crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

results.title: IDDE → Domestic crime group
results.description: Composite rate of domestic crimes vs IDDE
results.finding: Domestic: r=0.5948, p=0.0004. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
results.recommendation: If Domestic crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

results.title: IDDE → Large company share (DENUE)
results.description: Does digital development correlate with having more large companies?
results.finding: r(IDDE, large_share)=0.8229, p=0.0000.
results.recommendation: DENUE is completely unused in the dashboard. If significant, adds economic depth.

results.title: RANSAC robust: IDDE → Wage
results.description: RANSAC identifies outliers automatically and fits to inliers only
results.finding: OLS β=26.4, R²=0.371. RANSAC β=21.3, R²=0.248. Inliers=20, Outliers=11: .

results.title: RANSAC robust: IDDE → Crime
results.description: RANSAC identifies outliers automatically and fits to inliers only
results.finding: OLS β=13.9, R²=0.358. RANSAC β=8.6, R²=-0.015. Inliers=16, Outliers=15: .

results.title: Composite score → Crime
results.description: Average of 3 standardized variables (IDDE + employment + wages)

results.title: Composite score → Family trust
results.description: Average of 3 standardized variables (IDDE + employment + wages)

results.title: IDDE → Male victim share
results.description: Does digitalization correlate with the gender distribution of crime victims?
results.finding: r(IDDE, male_share)=-0.2253, p=0.2231.

results.title: Entropy-based: IDDE ↔ Crime
results.description: Normalized mutual information from discretized variables (captures nonlinear deps)
results.finding: MI=0.432, NMI=0.216 (normalized to [0,1]). Compare to Pearson r² from linear correlation.

results.title: Entropy-based: IDDE ↔ Wage
results.description: Normalized mutual information from discretized variables (captures nonlinear deps)
results.finding: MI=0.484, NMI=0.242 (normalized to [0,1]). Compare to Pearson r² from linear correlation.

results.title: Natural experiment: top vs bottom IDDE changers
results.description: Top 5 IDDE changers (Aguascal, Coahuila, Tlaxcala, Hidalgo, Ciudad d) vs bottom 5
results.finding: Top 5 crime Δ: -22.9. Bottom 5 crime Δ: -373.9. Diff: 351.0. Top IDDE changers did NOT reduce crime more.

results.title: Rank-rank: IDDE → Wage
results.description: Rank correlation removes scale effects; tests pure ordinal relationship

results.title: Rank-rank: IDDE → Crime
results.description: Rank correlation removes scale effects; tests pure ordinal relationship

results.title: Quantile regression: IDDE → Wage
results.description: Does the IDDE-wage relationship differ at the 25th vs 75th percentile?
results.finding: Q25: β=15.7; Q50: β=26.4; Q75: β=38.5. Spread=22.8. Effect is similar across quantiles.
results.recommendation: If spread is large, IDDE benefits high-wage states more (or less).

results.title: IDDE panel stationarity check
results.description: Is IDDE trending? If non-stationary, panel regression results may be spurious.
results.finding: Trend slope=0.897/year, R²=0.442, p=0.3348. Var(diff)/Var(level)=1.691. No clear trend. With only 4 years, formal ADF is unreliable.
results.recommendation: If IDDE is trending, panel FE might capture spurious trends. Acknowledge in defense doc.

results.title: Digital inequality (CV) → Crime
results.description: States with unbalanced digital development (high in some areas, low in others)
results.finding: r(digital_CV, crime)=-0.1836, p=0.3227. No significant relationship.
results.recommendation: If significant, the dashboard should warn that UNBALANCED digitalization is harmful.

results.title: IDDE → Employment (DENUE)
results.description: Total employment and employees-per-company vs IDDE
results.finding: Total employment: r=0.2747 (p=0.1348). Employees/company: r=0.7965 (p=0.0000).

results.title: IDDE → Violent crime group
results.description: Composite rate of violent crimes vs IDDE
results.finding: Violent: r=0.0088, p=0.9625. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
results.recommendation: If Violent crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

results.title: IDDE → Drug crime group
results.description: Composite rate of drug crimes vs IDDE
results.finding: Drug: r=0.3272, p=0.0723. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
results.recommendation: If Drug crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

results.title: Lagged difference: Δcrime(t) ~ ΔIDDE(t-1)
results.description: Dynamic panel approximation: does last year IDDE change predict this year crime change?
results.finding: β(ΔIDDE t-1)=1.7639, R²=0.0276, N=64 (state×year obs). Simple r(ΔIDDE_t-1, Δcrime_t)=0.0301, p=0.8132. No significant lagged effect.
results.recommendation: This is the closest we can get to Granger causality with 4 years.

results.title: Crime reduction leaders × IDDE
results.description: Which states reduced crime the most? Do they have high IDDE?
results.finding: r(IDDE, Δcrime)=0.1028, p=0.5821. Crime reducers: Campeche(-2150), Nuevo Le(-505), Baja Cal(-472), Durango(-442), Oaxaca(-339). IDDE does not predict crime reduction direction.

results.title: IDDE → Crime type concentration (HHI)
results.description: Herfindahl index of crime types: higher = more concentrated in few types
results.finding: r(IDDE, HHI)=0.1845, p=0.3203. No significant relationship.
